body_txt:
You have an Action Girl , who may also be One of the Boys . She rocks. But she's probably the only female in the main cast , or at very least, the most openly tomboyish in the female cast. And it doesn't seem to be playing well with the 18-35 male demographic.
So the writers, either on their own or because of Executive Meddling , soften her tough edge. Gradually, the Action Girl starts seeing significantly less action . She exchanges her armor for outfits that show off her figure. Her plotlines become increasingly centered around romance, dating, and fears that she isn't married yet. She is repeatedly captured and made helpless by the villains she handily thwarted in Season 2 just so the male characters can rescue her. The character who was once comfortable and competent has been Chickified. This trope is not merely an Action Girl who shows "female" emotions or likes girly-girl fashions : there is no rule that a heroine cannot show her feelings and neither is there a rule against kicking ass in a skirt or partaking in girlish pastimes . This trope is where a character becomes less action oriented at around the same time she becomes more openly girly. The character doesn't just put on a dress; she puts on a whole persona made of female stereotypes totally at odds from her previous behavior. This is about feminine maturity being equated with passivity , with "girls/women can't", rather than just learning to make wise choices or cope with challenges. If the heroine changes as the result of physical and/or emotional trauma, but maintains a strong presence in the narrative, it's more a case of Broken Bird , Break the Cutie , Heartbroken Badass , etc. Remember, Tropes Are Tools , and this is not a part of the Complaining Index either. Compare with Girliness Upgrade , in which a woman becomes more feminine but doesn't lose her edge. Remember, there is absolutely no overlap between these two tropes. If readers, watcher, players or critics perceive becoming more girly or womanly as less competent, that's on them, but if she's still getting the job done then it doesn't belong on this page. If this is done to a male character in a Slash Fic , it may be part of Ukefication or Aggressive Submissive . If the girl used to be an active character but now is just there, the trope is Men Act, Women Are . For girls that are both badass and feminine, see Girly Bruiser . For a tomboyish woman who still has a more girlish side, see Tomboy with a Girly Streak . For a girly girl who enjoys a rough pastime, see Girly Girl with a Tomboy Streak . See Faux Action Girl if the supposed female badass character never performed any such actions to begin with. When a female character wants to be more of an action girl but ends up being defied or discouraged from doing so, then it's Stay in the Kitchen . Contrast with Xenafication , which is the exact opposite. Not to be confused with Jack Chickification .
